text.title: product-development-trends-2026 Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for product-development-trends-2026 as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Confirmation echo: asked "is X still a trend in 2026?", agent answers yes by default without sourcing
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force the rubric — state in 4 values with citations, no narrative answer. "Yes" is not a valid rubric output.

failure-mode.description: Buzzword bloat: "AI-augmented", "data-driven", "cross-functional" expand into paragraphs of nothing
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cap each bucket's prose at at most 80 words in the snapshot. Length is inversely correlated with evidence density in trend writing.

failure-mode.description: Vendor-source bias: McKinsey/BCG/a16z articles promote their portfolio narrative
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require at least 1 community/OSS source (Stack Overflow, GitHub Octoverse) per bucket to balance the vendor-interest angle.

failure-mode.description: Cross-functional teams treated as a recipe rather than an org change
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cross-functional teams require structural org changes (reporting lines, incentives, co-location) — treating it as a methodology step produces vacuous prescriptions that agents cannot execute.
